rationale: The initial process (A0) uses a right-to-left override (RTLO) character in its filename, a common obfuscation technique. This process then initiates a network connection to an internal IP (EV-001) and spawns cmd.exe (EV-002), which subsequently spawns powershell.exe (EV-003). This chain of events, especially the RTLO character and the subsequent command execution and network activity, is highly indicative of malicious intent.
